headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the most negative attention, while Anthropic and Google Gemini leaned positive in a close tie, and Microsoft Copilot had limited evidence with a neutral-to-negative read [E1][E3][E4][E5].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was viewed negatively overall, with comments criticizing ChatGPT checkout performance and Codex lagging behind Claude Code [E1][E2][E4].
Microsoft Copilot had limited evidence, but the available comment was negative about GitHub Copilot’s reliability and frequent downtime [E5].
Anthropic skewed positive despite one critical coding review, because other comments praised Claude Code and Claude’s explanations for learning [E4][E6][E7].
Google Gemini received positive feedback from a user who said it saved substantial time and helped explain complex parts while researching components [E3].

evidence.excerpt: Walmart: ChatGPT checkout converted 3x worse than website

annotations.rationale: The title explicitly names ChatGPT and reports materially worse checkout conversion versus the website, indicating negative performance for OpenAI's commerce feature.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI is new to it, and if I had to guess, not that interested in getting good at it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says OpenAI is not very interested in improving this area, criticizing OpenAI's commitment/strategy around the checkout capability.

evidence.excerpt: I’m currently using Gemini to research components for a remote controlled plane. I have the frame of the plane and now need to buy correctly specced servo motors, an engine, battery, etc etc. It has saved me so much time and educated me tremendously on how the different components interact and the options available.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly credits Gemini with large time savings and strong educational value, which is strong praise for Gemini's usefulness and quality.

evidence.excerpt: Shocking how far ahead Claude Code is from Codex on the CLI front.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Claude Code is far ahead on CLI capabilities, which is strong comparative praise for Anthropic's product.
By saying Codex is far behind Claude Code on the CLI front, the commenter expresses a negative comparative view of OpenAI's product.

evidence.excerpt: I never use Github Copilot; it does go down a lot, if their status page is to be believed; I don't really care when it goes down

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly mentions GitHub Copilot and criticizes its frequent downtime, indicating negative sentiment about service reliability/availability.

evidence.excerpt: In my personal experience, claude needs heavy guidance and multiple rounds of feedback before arriving at a mergeable solution (if it does at all).

annotations.rationale: The commenter directly says Claude often requires heavy guidance and may fail to reach a mergeable solution, criticizing its coding quality/usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: Where I find it incredible - learning new things, I recently started flutter/dart dev - I just ask Claude to tell me about the bits, or explaining things to me, it's truly revolutionary imho

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly calls Claude 'incredible' and 'truly revolutionary' for learning and explanations, which is strong praise.
